File No. A-89-396
Page 2

agency had stated how they will vote on the variety of issues that
will reach the agency.

Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making,
participating in making, or otherwise using his or her official
position to influence a governmental decision in which the official has a financial interest. I advised you that although the
decision to appoint a replacement for the redevelopment agency was
a governmental decision, Councilmember Vernola could participate
provided it is not reasonably foreseeable that the decision would
have a material financial effect, distinguishable from its effect
on the public generally, on the official or a member of his or her
immediate family or on the official's financial interests. (Section 87103.) Absent a specific agreement between the
councilmember and his replacement that the latter will vote in a
particular manner on a particular decision, it is not reasonably
foreseeable that the appointment of a replacement will affect
Councilmember Vernola's financial interests.2/ (Benjamin Advice
Letter, No. A-86-148; Lofgren Advice Letter, No. A-86-307, copies
enclosed.)

If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact this office at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Counsel, Legal Division
KED:JWW:plh

Enclosures

___________________________________________________________________

2/ Of course once a replacement has been appointed, the
councilmember may in no way attempt to influence the decisions of
the agency as to specific decisions where the councilmember would
have a conflict of interest.

RICHARDS, WATSON & GERSHON
ATTORNEYS AT LAW
A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION

John Wallace, Esq.
Legal Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA    95804

Re:  Norwalk City Councilman Luigi Vernola

     Dear Mr. Wallace:

     Councilman Luigi Vernola has asked me to request
written confirmation of the oral advice you provided on May 11,
1989, when you advised me that Councilman Vernola could vote to
fill the existing vacancy on the Norwalk Redevelopment Agency.

     As you may recall, Councilman Vernola resigned from the
Norwalk Redevelopment Agency in December.  The City Council has
been unable to select a replacement, with two Councilmembers
supporting one candidate and the other two Councilmembers
supporting a second candidate.  Based on the attached letter from
the office of the former City Attorney, Councilman Vernola has
abstained from participation in selecting his replacement on
the Agency board.

     As a result of my research and our conversation on
May 11, 1989, I advised Councilman Vernola that he is not
financially interested in this decision and may participate in
selecting his replacement unless one of the candidates is, for
some reason, more likely to vote in a way that would increase or
decrease the value of Councilman Vernola's property located
within the Project Area.  Since there is no reasonable basis for
making such an assumption under the circumstances, I advised Mr.
Vernola that he can participate in this decision.

John Wallace, Esq.
June 29, 1989
Page Two


     In view of the existence of the previous opinion
finding that Mr. Vernola may not vote to select his replacement,
Mr. Vernola has asked for me written advice confirming your oral
advice.  Please contact me if you have any further questions or
require any additional information.


                                        Very truly yours,


                                        [signature]
                                        Steven L. Dorsey
                                        City Attorney
                                        City of Norwalk

Re:  Norwalk City Councilman Luigi Vernola

Dear Mr. Wallace:

Councilman Luigi Vernola has asked me to request
written confirmation of the oral advice you provided on May 11,
1989, when you advised me that Councilman Vernola could vote to
fill the existing vacancy on the Norwalk Redevelopment Agency.

As you may recall, Councilman Vernola resigned from the
Norwalk Redevelopment Agency in December.  The City Council has
been unable to select a replacement, with two Councilmembers
supporting one candidate and the other two Councilmembers
supporting a second candidate.  Based on the attached letter from
the office of the former City Attorney, Councilman Vernola has
abstained from participation in selecting his replacement on the
Agency board.

As a result of my research and our conversation on
May 11, 1989, I advised Councilman Vernola that he is not
financially interested in this decision and may participate in
selecting his replacement unless one of the candidates is, for
some reason, more likely to vote in a way that would increase or
decrease the value of Councilman Vernola's property located
within the Project Area.  Since there is no reasonable basis for
making such an assumption under the circumstances, I advised Mr.

John Wallace, Esq.
June 29, 1989

     In view of the existence of the previous opinion

fppcvern.nor

July 6, 1989


Steven L. Dorsey
City Attorney - Norwalk
Richards, Watson & Gershon
333 So. Hope Street, 38th Floor
Los Angeles, CA  90071-1469

Re: Letter No. 89-396

Dear Mr. Dorsey:

We received your letter requesting confirmation of advice
under the Political Reform Act on July 3, 1989. Your letter has
been assigned to John Wallace for response. If you have any
questions, you may contact him directly at (916) 322-5901.

If the letter is appropriate for confirmation without further
analysis, we will attempt to expedite our response. A confirming
response will be released after it has gone through our approval
process. If the letter is not appropriate for this treatment, the
staff person assigned to prepare the response will contact you
shortly to advise you. In such cases, the normal analysis, review
and approval process will be followed.

You should be aware that your letter and our response are
public records which may be disclosed to any interested person
upon receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel
